channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Family looks for missing Broadway dancer Zelig Williams in South Carolina
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — A family in South Carolina pleaded for help Wednesday in finding Broadway dancer Zelig Williams, whose disappearance nearly two weeks ago has also drawn the concern of the broader theater community, including Hugh Jackman.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Texas man facing execution in shaken baby syndrome case awaits clemency ruling
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — A Texas man who this week could be the first person in the U.S. executed for a murder conviction tied to the diagnosis of shaken baby syndrome awaited a decision Wednesday on his request for clemency from a state board.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Listeria recall grows to 12 million pounds of meat and poultry, some of it sent to US schools
item.description: A nationwide recall of meat and poultry products potentially contaminated with listeria bacteria has expanded to nearly 12 million pounds.
item.dc:creator: Jonel Aleccia, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: US Navy fighter jet with two on board crashes during training in Washington state
item.description: The U.S. Navy was searching Wednesday for two aviators whose plane crashed during a routine training flight. The EA-18G Growler jet from the Electronic Attack Squadron crashed east of Mount Rainier at about 3:23 p.m. Tuesday, according to Whidbey Island Naval Air Station.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Biden to deliver eulogy during Ethel Kennedy memorial service in DC
item.description: Ethel Kennedy, 96, died last week. She had been hospitalized after suffering a stroke earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Charges filed against Utah woman accused of murdering, burying husband
item.description: Gledhill allegedly put Johnson's body in a rooftop storage container and slid it down the stairs and into the back of their minivan, the informant said.
item.dc:creator: Aubree B. Jennings, NewsNation

item.title: A worker at a Crimean wildlife park has been killed by lions
item.description: TALLINN, Estonia (AP) — A worker at a wildlife park in Crimea died Wednesday when she was attacked by lions, officials said. According to the Russian Investigative Committee's office in the Russia-annexed peninsula, the death occurred when the worker went into a cage at the Taigan park to clean it.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Navy: Fighter jet with two on board crashed in Washington state during training
item.description: The U.S. Navy was searching for two aviators who went missing after their plane crashed during a routine training flight in Washington state.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Secret Service trained at mock White House that Hollywood producer Tyler Perry built as stage
item.description: The U.S. Secret Service used Tyler Perry's mock White House in Atlanta for a training scenario in 2023 as it works to construct its own White House training facility.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 102 arrested along Jordan River Trail in 30-day police operation
item.description: The Salt Lake City Police Department announced that 102 people were arrested in a 30-day "enforcement operation.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, NewsNation

item.title: Nebraska court allows voter registrations from people with felony convictions
item.description: Nebraskans with felony convictions may register to vote after the state's highest court ruled Wednesday that a top election official had no authority to deem a law restoring those rights unconstitutional.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KREX 5

item.title: FTC announces rule to make it easier to cancel subscriptions
item.description: The department will soon implement a "final click to cancel rule," a move that will require sellers to make it "as easy for consumers to cancel their enrollment as it was to sign up.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, WFLA, NBC

item.title: FEMA workers threatened by armed group in Tennessee
item.description: "They were all armed, open carry not guns drawn, but they had surrounded them and there was a lady there that was yelling and threatening them.
item.dc:creator: Karen Jenkins, ABC 4

item.title: FBI says ISIS was behind foiled mass shooting plot on Election Day
item.description: Nasir Ahmad Tawhedi, 27, was connected with an ISIS contact he knew as "Malik" while planning a foiled terror plot.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Michelle Obama will headline an Atlanta rally aimed at boosting voter turnout
item.description: Former first lady Michelle Obama is headlining a rally in Atlanta alongside celebrities and local civic leaders focusing on engaging younger and first-time voters, as well as voters of color.
item.dc:creator: Matt Brown, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Menendez brothers' family to push for their release as prosecutors review 1989 case
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The extended family of Erik and Lyle Menendez will advocate for the brothers' release from prison during a news conference set for Wednesday in downtown Los Angeles as prosecutors review new evidence to determine whether they should be serving life sentences for killing their pare
item.dc:creator: STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Republicans challenge more than 63,000 voters in Georgia, but few removed, AP finds
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — From Georgia's mountains to its Atlantic shore, challenges to the qualifications of voters have rolled in this summer and fall, part of a wide-ranging national effort coordinated by Donald Trump’s allies to enlist Republican activists to remove people they view as suspect from the vot
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY and CHARLOTTE KRAMON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Hurricane fallout threatens to hinder voting
item.description: The fallout from a pair of deadly hurricanes threatens to roil voting in the Southeast with complications for election officials and residents alike in states key to this year’s presidential race.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump: Black and Hispanic voters who vote for Harris 'gotta have your head examined'
item.description: The former president suggested any Black or Hispanic voters who do not support him should have their head examined, echoing a similar comment he has made about Jewish voters backing Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: California man accused of assaulting, tying up homeowner during burglary
item.description: A California man was arrested on accusations that he assaulted a man and tied him up in his own home during a home invasion in Riverside County.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Prosecutor to 'review' Marilyn Manson rape allegations after victim calls DA out weeks before election
item.description: Los Angeles investigators have "new evidence" in connection with rape allegations against Marilyn Manson from former "Game of Thrones" actress Esme Bianco.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Adobe starts roll-out of AI video tools, challenging OpenAI and Meta
item.description: Facing much larger rivals, Adobe has staked its future on building models trained on data that it has rights to use, ensuring the output can be legally used in commercial work.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Mexico's former public security chief set to be sentenced in US drug case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Mexico's former public security chief is set to be sentenced in a U.S. court on Wednesday after being convicted of taking bribes to aid drug traffickers.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 19 mayoral candidates compete to lead Portland, Oregon, in a race with homelessness at its heart
item.description: PORTLAND, Ore. (AP) — A lot of political changes are coming to Portland, Oregon. The open mayoral race in the state’s most populous city features 19 candidates — all vying to lead after years of growing frustration over homeless encampments.
item.dc:creator: CLAIRE RUSH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Unions face a moment of truth in Michigan in this year's presidential race
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris and former President Donald Trump are appealing to middle-class workers in very different ways.
item.dc:creator: Joey Cappelletti And Matt Brown, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Former Ozzy Osbourne guitarist Jake E. Lee shot while walking dog in Las Vegas
item.description: Guitarist Jake E. Lee, who formerly played for Ozzy Osbourne, was shot Tuesday while walking his dog, his publicist said.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KTLA 5

item.title: Prosecutors will not file criminal charges against 2 people at center of Los Angeles racism scandal
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The Los Angeles city attorney’s office said Tuesday that it does not plan to file criminal charges against two people who were investigated in connection with the unlawful recording of a racist conversation that rocked City Hall and prompted the city council president to resign af
item.dc:creator: STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Hundreds of troops kicked out under 'don't ask, don't tell' get upgraded to honorable discharges
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Pentagon announced Tuesday that more than 800 military personnel have seen their service records upgraded to honorable discharges after previously being kicked out of the military under its former “don’t ask, don’t tell” policy.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Idaho will begin using deep veins as backup for lethal injection executions, officials say
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — Idaho will begin using central veins deep in the groin, neck, chest or arm for executions by lethal injection if attempts to insert standard IV lines fail, the Idaho Department of Correction announced Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: North Carolina man shot, killed outside of his own wedding; family wants answers
item.description: Family members say that he briefly left the venue to retrieve something and was followed by a man who accused him of cutting him off on the road when he came back.
item.dc:creator: Elijah Skipper, NewsNation

item.title: Former Ozzy Osbourne guitarist Jake E. Lee shot multiple times in Las Vegas
item.description: Las Vegas police said in an emailed statement that the victim was shot around 2:40 a.m. Tuesday and was taken to a hospital for treatment.
item.dc:creator: MARIA SHERMAN, RIO YAMAT and ANDREW DALTON, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Former owner of dog found chained to pole ahead of Hurricane Milton arrested, to 'face the music,' DA says
item.description: The owner of a dog found chained to a pole during a hurricane has been identified and could face animal cruelty charges, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court turns down Michael Avenatti’s appeal of Stormy Daniels identity theft conviction
item.description: The Supreme Court on Tuesday declined to hear disgraced lawyer Michael Avenatti’s appeal of his aggravated identity theft conviction for stealing book proceeds from porn actor Stormy Daniels.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Single mother identified as New Jersey train operator killed in crash with tree
item.description: Its operator, 41-year-old Jessica Haley of Levittown, Pennsylvania, died while nearly two dozen passengers were injured.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Yakubovsky, NewsNation

item.title: Rufus Wainwright blasts Trump playing 'Hallelujah' at town hall: 'The height of blasphemy'
item.description: The 51-year-old singer said Tuesday that he was "horrified" to learn his 2001 cover of Leonard Cohen's "Hallelujah" was part of the playlist at the Trump campaign event a night earlier.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, Fox 5

item.title: Walgreens to close 1,200 US stores
item.description: The plan announced Tuesday includes the closing of 300 stores that had been approved under a previous cost-cutting plan.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Rising chocolate prices impact Halloween candy choices
item.description: Despite rising chocolate and candy prices, Americans are expected to spend $3.5 million on Halloween sweets this year.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, WFLA, NBC
